Football.title: Liam Rosenior reveals Chelsea transfer talks after Enzo Fernandez and Cole Palmer claims
Football.content:
Liam Rosenior reveals Chelsea transfer talks after Enzo Fernandez and Cole Palmer claims

Both Enzo Fernandez and Cole Palmer have been linked with moves away from Chelsea ahead of the summer transfer window and boss Liam Rosenior has weighed in on their respective futures

View 2 Images Liam Rosenior has already held talks over the summer transfer window (Image: Getty Images)

Liam Rosenior has revealed that Chelsea’s transfer planning is already underway amid speculation over the futures of Cole Palmer and Enzo Fernandez. Both Blues stars have been linked with moves away from Stamford Bridge in the summer.

‌



England star Palmer has been linked with Manchester United, while Fernandez put his future in doubt with some particularly non-committal comments. Asked about Chelsea’s plans for the upcoming summer transfer window, boss Rosenior has confirmed that early talks are underway.

‌



“We’re having really detailed conversations now about the areas that we want to strengthen,” Rosenior said.

‌



“I’ve had great conversations about what I think we need. We’re very aligned, actually, in terms of where we want to strengthen and how we go about that, which is very exciting. The most important bit, though, is making sure we get the now right as well.

“It’s great to be able to plan for the future but I want to make sure we’re also in a good place. We want to win. That’s why I’m here, and I’m having really good conversations with the ownership and sporting directors about that.”

FOLLOW OUR CHELSEA FB PAGE! Latest Blues news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Enzo Fernandez undermines Liam Rosenior again with brutal Chelsea manager comments READ MORE :

Premier League clubs to vote on handing VAR more power after World Cup decision READ MORE :

On Palmer and Fernandez, Rosenior has confirmed that he has no doubts when it comes to their commitment. He added: “I have a very, very good relationship with the players because we speak all the time.

“Not just about football, or the situation with their contracts, but about their life, with their kids, how their kids are, how school is. I have a very, very close relationship with Enzo and with the other players.

"In terms of contracts and where they want to be, there's not one player at this club since I've been here that has said that he doesn't want to be here in the summer.

‌



“In fact, the conversations are more about how we improve, what we can do as a group to improve, what we need to do to win in this moment. And that's where we are at the moment.”

View 2 Images Both Cole Palmer and Enzo Fernandez have been linked with moves away from Chelsea (Image: Getty Images)

Chelsea are in a battle to secure a spot in the Champions League for next season. They currently sit sixth in the table ahead of the weekend’s showdown with Everton. Rosenior has said that Chelsea’s planning is not dependent on qualification for the competition.

‌



“In my conversations with the ownership and the sporting directors, our plans are not solely based on whether we make the Champions League or not,” Rosenior said.

“But there's so many ifs. There's so many ifs in life. So we just have to make sure that the 'if' that we create is as positive as possible and we're still in a position to do that.”

Football.title: Liverpool have key advantage over PSG in Champions League that Chelsea were denied
Football.content:
Liverpool have key advantage over PSG in Champions League that Chelsea were denied

Paris Saint-Germain thrashed Chelsea 8-2 in the Champions League last 16 after getting a Ligue 1 fixture postponed, and they face Liverpool next in Europe

View 2 Images Arne Slot's Liverpool will be out for revenge against PSG in the Champions League (Image: UEFA via Getty Images)

Paris Saint-Germain are reportedly unlikely to have their fixture against Lens postponed between the two legs of their Champions League encounter with Liverpool. PSG meet the Reds in the quarter-finals next month with the first leg in Paris on April 8.

‌



The French champions benefited from five days' rest between both legs of their last 16 tie against Chelsea this month after their request to postpone a Ligue 1 fixture against Nantes was granted. Luis Enrique's side were scheduled to face struggling Nantes on the weekend of March 13-15, sandwiched between their matches against Chelsea on Wednesday, March 11 and Tuesday, March 17.

‌



However, that match was rescheduled to April 22 instead, allowing them to concentrate fully on the knockout tie against Liam Rosenior's side. That decision proved fruitful with PSG demolishing the Blues 8-2 across both legs.

‌



Graeme Souness savages Mo Salah and demands he LEAVES Liverpool READ MORE :

Many have questioned whether PSG will secure the same arrangement for their last eight encounter with Arne Slot's team. The Parisians are scheduled to face title contenders Lens on April 11 - sandwiched between a two-legged tie against the Reds.

However, French football expert Matt Spiro suggests the European champions will not receive that same privilege against Liverpool. He claims Lens will not be entertaining any requests for postponement and continues by suggesting that the fixture will stay in its current slot as no alternative arrangement would 'make sense' for Lens.

PSG presently top the French league table by a single point and hold a game in hand over second-placed Lens. Liverpool will be hoping to head into that clash against Enrique's outfit with momentum after demolishing Turkish outfit Galatasaray 4-0 on Wednesday evening (4-1 on aggregate) to advance.

‌



FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

However, they face a challenging FA Cup quarter-final away at Manchester City just four days prior to the first leg in Paris. Despite this, Slot maintains he is relishing the prospect of taking on the side that eliminated his team on penalties in the last 16 last season.

He said: "If you go to latter stages of Champions League then you know on thing for sure – you're going to face Paris Saint-Germain because they are an incredible team. We have experienced that last season when we faced them away from home, very, very, very difficult game, here at Anfield we played a fantastic game.

‌



Watch Liverpool vs Galatasaray on TNT Sports This article contains affiliate links, we will receive a commission on any sales we generate from it. Learn more £30.99 TNT Sports Get TNT Sports here Product Description Liverpool have reached the UEFA Champions League round of 16 where they face Galatasaray on March 18 . Fans can watch the game on the TNT Sports Prime Video channel.

"I've said a lot of times how great a match that was, the best game I've managed in my career. Not the result, but it was the way football should be played, both teams just playing football, just wanting to entertain the fans.

"We were the only team that brought them to extra-time, we were the only team that brought them to penalties. That team has shown this season they haven't dropped a level, and we showed tonight [against Galatasaray] we can still perform at the level we were performing for large parts of last season.

‌



View 2 Images Liverpool were beaten on penalties by PSG last season (Image: Getty Images)

"It gives me and us a lot of confidence that we have this performance. But it's not first one this season, we've had more of them especially in Europe, and we have to try to find the consistency.

"Although I can already disappoint people, this performance is hardly possible to copy one more time because 5.02 xG on a Champions League night whilst conceding 0.18, that's not going to be easy to copy that one more time."

Article continues below

Football.title: 'I left Sunderland for Newcastle – the dressing rooms difference explained why we'd win'
Football.content:
'I left Sunderland for Newcastle – the dressing rooms difference explained why we'd win'

This midfielder was a Sunderland academy product and scored in a Tyne-Wear derby before making a contentious switch to Newcastle in 2014

View 2 Images Jack Colback has shed light on his move from Sunderland to Newcastle (Image: In The Mixer)

Jack Colback has revealed that he quickly understood why he had consistently triumphed over Newcastle as a Sunderland player after changing sides. The 36-year-old, a product of the Black Cats' academy, made a controversial switch to the Magpies in 2014.

‌



Despite being a childhood Newcastle supporter, Colback joined their fierce rivals at the tender age of eight and ascended to first-team contention. He made 135 appearances for them before becoming the first player in nearly two decades to cross the divide and sign for Newcastle.

‌



Upon his arrival at St James' Park, Colback remembers swiftly realising that during that era, a lack of team spirit often resulted in his former side, Sunderland, outperforming their rivals in the Tyne-Wear Derby, the 159th edition of which will kick off at 12pm on Sunday.

‌



You can listen to brand new episodes of In The Mixer on Spotify and Apple Podcasts!

Speaking on In The Mixer brought to you by Sky Bet, Colback said: "I'll always remember Steven Taylor was the first lad to come and make an effort really. I appreciated that, especially from him. He was diehard Newcastle.

"So if anyone was going to dislike me as an ex-Sunderland player it was him. He was good. Tim Krul, another one, really good. But the dressing room there wasn't great when I entered it.

‌



"As soon as I experienced a week or two, I kind of understood why we always used to beat them in the derby. Because it wasn't together, it was very much a group here and a group there."

During his time at Sunderland, Colback featured in seven derbies, securing three victories, three draws and suffering just one defeat. He added: "There were a lot of French lads [at Newcastle], probably 11 or 12 at the time.

"Nothing against French people, but they were kind of... You'd hear little whispers and stuff and it would drag everyone into a negative circle a bit. It was one of those where I thought I can understand why we used to win those games."

‌



Colback also reflected on one of his early derbies as a Newcastle player, when he was substituted due to injury following a challenge from former colleague Lee Cattermole. He recalled: "It was at the Stadium of Light, we actually started really well and I was playing well and enjoying myself, getting on the ball.

"But I ended up going off injured in the tackle with Cattermole. It was a fine tackle, I twisted my knee, it wasn't him who went through me. It looks like he's gone, 'Have that, you Geordie t**t.'

‌



"But no, he was a great lad, someone you'd want in your team. Me wounded on the ground like I'd been harpooned wasn't a fair reflection. But the worst thing was, because I got injured and I needed to come off, I had to come off the other side of the pitch.

View 2 Images Colback was sidelined for six weeks after the derby (Image: Getty)

"I had to walk all the way around the back of the goal. It was like a Mexican wave of abuse. I could see fans almost racing to come down. They made their feelings known.

Article continues below

"It almost motivated me. I always preferred the derby game away from home because of the abuse you'd get. Loved it. Turning up to St James' [with Sunderland], you're under a tunnel a bit in the bus and there'd be thousands of fans there already going, 'Scum, scum, scum.' That's what we live for as players, I think."

JACK COLBACK EPISODE ON YOUTUBE: Subscribe now to be the first to watch the latest episodes of In The Mixer and other original shows, brought to you by Sky Bet. Watch All Out Football's episode with Jack Colback here.

Football.title: Andoni Iraola clarifies Bournemouth future as he's backed for Man Utd job
Football.content:
Andoni Iraola clarifies Bournemouth future as he's backed for Man Utd job

Bournemouth boss Andoni Iraola has been linked with a summer move to Manchester United and addressed his future after Friday evening’s draw with the Red Devils

View 2 Images Andoni Iraola has been linked with a move to Bilbao (Image: Getty Images)

Bournemouth boss Andoni Iraola assures he is not distracted by links with former club Athletic Bilbao. The Spanish side are on the hunt for a new manager, with current head coach Ernesto Valverde having announced he will leave at the end of the season.

‌



Iraola, 43, is an iconic figure at the Basque side, having made 510 appearances for the club during his playing career. He is also out of contract at the end of the season, which could make a move a lot easier.

‌



But Iraola insists that he is not letting talk over his future detract from the work he is doing on the South Coast.

‌



Speaking in his post-match press conference, the Bournemouth boss said: “It affects me probably as a supporter because, I support this club.

“Obviously I've been playing there for a lot of years and has been a surprise also for me. I have a lot of friends there, and in your group, you see the news and they send you their messages.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Bournemouth boss strongly disagrees with Michael Carrick on Man Utd controversy READ MORE :

Fuming Michael Carrick calls out referee for 'astonishing' decision in Man Utd draw READ MORE :

“And I can only speak very well. It's my biggest reference in football, Ernesto. So it's a decision that he has made, but it's not affecting me apart from the supporter side, let's say.”

Iraola then confirmed that he had not been approached by anyone at Bilbao and suggested that talks remain open about extending his stay with the Cherries.

He added: “I think there are situations that I said a lot of times, we have to discuss with the club internally, but it's not about any other club, it's about Bournemouth, and these are the only discussions that we are having.”

‌



While Iraola has been linked with his former side, United have been touted as potential suitors for him in the future. And Jamie Redknapp has given the green light to a move to Old Trafford.

View 2 Images Ernesto Valverde is set to leave Spanish side Athletic Bilbao (Image: Getty Images)

Speaking on Sky Sports, he said: “I’ve said that before absolutely. He could definitely do it. I love the way that Bournemouth play. They play in a high energy tempo that I think would be what Manchester United would want.

‌



“If he was one of the managers they looked it, he could definitely do that job. There will be other jobs in the summer as well, obviously Tottenham will be available.

“There’s going to be a lot of clubs for him. It’s just a case of what’s right for him. He’s done such a great job at Bournemouth. I hope he stays because I love the club but I can also see the opportunities there.”

Meanwhile, Andy Cole also believes Iraola would be a good candidate for his former side. He said: “Yes, why not? He’s a good manager. Put it this way, he’s not going to struggle for options. To get Bournemouth in a position they’re in playing really good football, bringing in good players.

Article continues below

“That’s a big bonus. I’ve watched them a lot this season, they’re a good team. Finishing mid-table again for such a small club is testament to him.”

Football.title: Inside Espanyol's La Liga resurgence with new owners as club fight for European places
Football.content:
Inside Espanyol's La Liga resurgence with new owners as club fight for European places

Espanyol's impressive second campaign back in La Liga has been one of the stories of the season in the Spanish top flight

View 3 Images Espanyol have been the surprise package in La Liga this season (Image: Getty Images)

Espanyol’s incredible rise has seen the side go from the second tier of Spanish football to being on the cusp of qualifying for Europe in less than two years. A dramatic play-off final in June 2024 saw the club beat Real Oviedo 2-1 on aggregate to seal a return to La Liga, marking a second promotion to the top flight in three years.

‌



Their first season back in La Liga was always going to be a tricky affair and it proved just that, with Los Periquitos narrowly avoiding relegation under the management of Manolo Gonzalez. Yet, despite having a very limited budget to work with, Espanyol were able to build on their survival by making several shrewd signings last summer.

‌



They were given an extra boost when, after a strong start to the 2025/26 campaign, Velocity Sports Partners (VSP) - the sports investment arm of Burnley FC owners ALK Capital - completed their takeover of the Catalan club last October.

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Gonzalez’s men went on to secure five straight league victories between November and December, moving the La Liga underdogs to within two points of fourth place. Since then, Espanyol have endured a difficult run of form which has seen them fail to win any of their last 11 league fixtures.

However, they are still in eighth place - just four points off a Europa Conference League place with 10 games left to play, which would have been seen as a huge success at the start of the season.

‌



Former Man City star puts call out to English players to join Marcus Rashford in La Liga READ MORE :

With the players and coaching staff’s dreams within touching distance, Unai Ezkurra, Director of Football Processes and Big Data at Espanyol, shared his thoughts on the club’s sudden turnaround this season, by explaining their philosophy.

Ezkurra said: “We’re very pleased with the performance in the first part of the season, especially of the squad. For us, maybe it was a bit of an exceptional start with regards to our performance, to be honest, if we analyse it overall.

“Sometimes you get the points you don't deserve and sometimes you don't you don't get the ones you deserve. So at the end, I think we are now rebalancing a little bit the performance of the season.”

‌



He explained: “The strategy was clear when I got to the club with [sporting director] Fran Garagarza. There was a plan to re-establish the club with some stability after two relegations in the last few years. We achieved promotion last year with difficulties in terms of finances, readjusting with the La Liga salary cap.

View 3 Images Manolo Gonzalez's side are eighth in the table (Image: Getty Images)

“This season was also another step forward to grow. For us, it’s very important to grow organically. I think, in football, you cannot buy success or progress. Our idea is to grow every year, making a stronger structural base of a club that can establish itself and fight for this moment.

‌



“We are in a situation where we are thinking, ‘Why not challenge for the European spots?’ But also with a clear vision to keep growing organically.”

Bradley Spiby, the club’s Chief Operating Officer (COO) and formerly Burnley’s Chief of Staff between February 2024 and December 2025, highlighted the ambitions of the new ownership group.

He said: “I think it goes without saying that this club has historically been very successful. Something we talk a lot about in our ownership group is being custodians. It’s something that we wear very heavily on our shoulders, not just here, but also in Burnley, which is another historic founding club of the league in the UK.

‌



Sky Sports discounted Premier League and EFL package This article contains affiliate links, we will receive a commission on any sales we generate from it. Learn more £43 £35 Sky Get the deal here Product Description Sky has slashed the price of its Essential TV and Sky Sports bundle ahead of the 2025/26 season, saving members £192 and offering more than 1,400 live matches across the Premier League, EFL and more. Sky will show at least 215 live Premier League games next season, an increase of up to 100 more.

“When we think about being custodians, it really is our responsibility to lean on and preserve what has made Espanyol what it is today historically and use that as a superpower going forward. Our goal is to make sure we return to that status with a self-sufficient and stable base so it can continue to be that for a very long time.

“We're fortunate that the previous owners have provided us with a very strong foundation from a financial perspective to work from. It's our job now to utilise the assets that the club has at its fingertips, which I think are really difficult to find across European football.”

‌



View 3 Images Espanyol's mentality has shifted this season (Image: Getty Images)

That same attitude and ambition extends to the players. Englishman Tyrhys Dolan, who was signed on a free transfer from Blackburn Rovers last summer, explained the new mindset and the drive of the players this season.

Dolan said: “For me, when I came to Espanyol, it was just off the back of last season and it was a proper survivor’s mentality. Everyone was really willing to fight and one thing I noticed was that the supporters were really behind us with everything we were doing because obviously Barcelona is so big in the city.

Article continues below

“Everyone who's rocking with Espanyol is so loyal to us. So I knew when I came here that the supporters had our backs. Obviously, the start to life here was amazing and probably unexpected for a lot of people going off the back of the previous year. So it was nothing but positive for me.

“When we started racking up a few wins, it was really understanding a winner's mindset in the team and the manager implemented that into us. You know, he's mental about football, and that was something that I was excited about. We’re just enjoying it and I’m just taking it all in my stride as I go.”

UK fans can watch LALIGA live throughout this season via Premier Sports, or on Disney+ for each Saturday 8pm kick off

Football.title: Italian media in disagreement with Antonio Conte over Scott McTominay performance
Football.content:
Italian media in disagreement with Antonio Conte over Scott McTominay performance

Former Manchester United midfielder Scott McTominay was decisive for Napoli in their Serie A encounter with Cagliari on Friday evening

View 3 Images Scott McTominay scored for Napoli on Friday evening (Image: Ivan Romano, Getty Images)

Scott McTominay's second-minute goal against Cagliari was crucial for Napoli as the Serie A defending champions climbed into second place on Friday night. The former Manchester United midfielder once again played the hero, his early strike securing all three points for Antonio Conte's team.

‌



The Scot, who moved to the Serie A side from United in August 2024, reacted swiftly to score after the ball had rebounded off the post, following a corner. Although Napoli had to work hard for their victory and struggled to increase their lead, they did manage their first clean sheet since January 4.

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Ravaged by injuries, Conte managed to pair McTominay with ex-Man City star Kevin De Bruyne, who helped drive Gli Azzurri to victory.

However, the former Chelsea boss wasn't entirely pleased with McTominay, or his team overall. He said: "[Frank] Anguissa and McTominay aren't 'brilliant' again yet, Kevin [De Bruyne] I thought was doing well. [Stanislav] Lobotka struggled a little bit more."

Here, Mirror Football takes a closer look at how McTominay's performance was perceived by the press, with some sections disagreeing with Conte's assessment of the game.

‌



Bournemouth boss strongly disagrees with Michael Carrick on Man Utd controversy READ MORE :

Furious Bruno Fernandes calls out VAR as Man Utd question two penalty decisions vs Bournemouth READ MORE :

Il Napolista

Il Napolista awarded the Scot a seven out of 10, emphasising how his goal and overall input outweighed any shortcomings in his performance. They said: "He takes the game and the three points, as he did so many times last season. And this time he does it right away, before he's even started. For this reason, he's forgiven for all the balls he subsequently lost."

They also lauded the 29-year-old's leadership skills, attributing the victory to his experience. They added: "The start, however, was Napoli-like. Ready, go, goal after seventy seconds.

"The Scotsman is the true leader of this team. He moves with a magical aura. With a princely air. He started high up, close to De Bruyne. Then, in the second half, Conte moved him back. And he changed the score without batting an eyelid."

‌



Eurosport Italy

Eurosport gave McTominay a 7.5 out of ten. They wrote: "Here's the Scot, back in fine form: his early-game goal certainly wasn't as beautiful or crucial as the overhead kick that gave Napoli the Scudetto about a year ago. However, it was a significant goal against a tough team. Antonio Conte is delighted with this player's return."

View 3 Images Scott McTominay in action against Cagliari

Corriere dello Sport

The Italian publication praised McTominay for taking the lead early on. They said: "McTominay right away. Napoli took the lead after just over a minute: a Politano corner sparked a scramble in the box, with Beukema laying it off, Buongiorno, despite missing his shot, hitting the post, and McTominay coming in to tap in.

‌



"Conte's side created another opportunity in the 6th minute, with a Politano free kick parried away by Caprile, and once more through McTominay, whose header was tipped narrowly over the bar by Mina. A scrappy triumph, more attributable to tension than performance, but vital for maintaining their Champions League challenge."

View 3 Images Sections of the Italian press disagreed with Napoli coach Antonio Conte (Image: Getty Images)

Sport Napoli

Sport Napoli lauded the former United star for his sharp instincts in securing the early strike, though they were harsh on Napoli's general display. They wrote: "McTominay was decisive at the start of the race. An early goal from Scott McTominay was enough to defeat the Rossoblu 1-0 and secure three crucial points in the race for the top spot in the table.

Article continues below

"It was a start that seemed to be shaping the game, but in reality, it belied the difficulties that would emerge as the minutes passed. Napoli built the game, controlled the ball, and came close to doubling their lead several times, but suffered a certain inaccuracy in their final passes and in front of goal."

Football.title: Graeme Souness savages Mo Salah and demands he LEAVES Liverpool
Football.content:
Graeme Souness savages Mo Salah and demands he LEAVES Liverpool

Both Liverpool and Mohamed Salah have endured difficult seasons with Reds great Graeme Souness having offered a particularly scathing take on the Egyptian’s form and his future at Anfield

View 2 Images Graeme Souness has offered his take on Mo Salah's form (Image: Getty Images)

Liverpool icon Graeme Souness believes Mo Salah should end his Anfield career THIS summer, after blaming the Egyptian for the Reds’ downturn. The reigning Premier League champions have endured a difficult season under Arne Slot, having slipped to fifth.

‌



They could still finish the season with a top four spot and two pieces of silverware - the FA Cup and the Champions League - but it has been a very tricky title defence.

‌



Salah has also had a tricky season in front of goal, having scored just five times in the Premier League. Explaining his form to Mail Online, Reds hero Souness said: “What happens, in my opinion and in my experience, is your career happens one of two ways.

‌



"When you get to that sort of 33-34 age area, it's either falling off a cliff, or it's a slow decline. I think Salah is the former.

“I went to see the very first game of the season, the Community Shield against Crystal Palace, and I was sitting next to my young son, I said, ‘I don’t know what’s wrong with him?’ And then I thought, maybe he needs half a dozen games to get up to speed.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Liverpool hit with Alisson injury blow as replacement decision made for Brighton READ MORE :

“But he's never improved. His numbers tell you that. His numbers are way down, and he is the single biggest reason why Liverpool are having an indifferent season. Because within that group of players, there'll be some that are feeling the pain, some are feeling the negativity that's around the place, and they're not responding very well to it.

“And when you're in that position, you need big players to stand up and be counted. And I think that's been a bit of a shock to Liverpool supporters, the manager and coaching staff, that those guys have not done that.”

Souness continued his tear down of Salah by claiming that he is the main reason behind the Reds’ difficult season. And asked if the forward should leave this summer, he simply replied: “Yes.”

‌



View 2 Images Mohamed Salah has scored just five Premier League goals this season (Image: Getty Images)

Souness added: “His best is behind him now, there’s no doubt about that. The first person to know that is yourself, and it happens to everyone. I don't see the same sparkle. I think if you're looking for the single biggest reason (for Liverpool’s shaky form), it's because he's not been getting the numbers.

“He's been for six, seven years the Liverpool go-to man. He has completely changed games when it wasn't going their way, and he has scored a ridiculous number of goals every season. He’s a great - a Liverpool great - but his best season is behind him.”

Article continues below

Salah remains under contract with Liverpool until 2027, having only penned a new deal with Liverpool in April last year.

Football.title: O'Sullivan through to World Open final after deciding-frame thriller
Football.content:
Ronnie O'Sullivan: Seven-time world snooker champion through to World Open final in deciding-frame thriller against Wu Yize

Ronnie O'Sullivan made it through to World Open final after a sensational deciding-frame clearance

Ronnie O'Sullivan made it through to the World Open final after clinching a deciding-frame thriller against Wu Yize.

With O'Sullivan and Wu locked at 5-5 in the race to six frames, 'The Rocket' produced a wonderful 89 counter-clearance from 43-0 down to seal a dramatic 6-5 victory.

The seven-time world champion is through to a 66th ranking final and now has a chance to win a record-extending 42nd ranking title in Sunday's final against Judd Trump or Thepchaiya Un-Nooh.

Ronnie O’Sullivan: The GOAT

🏆 7-time World Champion



🏆 8-time UK Champion



🏆 8-time Masters Champion



🏆 41 Ranking Titles



⭐️ 1,320 Century Breaks



⭐️ 17 Maximum Breaks



⭐️ Highest Break: 153



⏳ Fastest Maximum Break in 5’08’’, unbeaten for 29 years



🐐 The Greatest of All Time



On Friday, O'Sullivan made the highest break in professional snooker by hitting a 153 at the tournament.

The 50-year-old achieved the feat after leaving quarter-final opponent Ryan Day in a snooker at the start of the opening frame and the Welshman's failed attempt to get out of it handed O'Sullivan a free ball.

That allowed the seven-time world champion to commence his break with the green, followed by his opening black to put him on eight points before even beginning on the reds.

O'Sullivan then reeled off the 15 reds, accompanied by 13 further blacks and two pinks before clearing up the colours to land a record-breaking 153, paving the way for a dominant 5-0 victory.

O'Sullivan's route to World Open final

Football.title: Getawhisky faces Charisma Cat at Newbury
Football.content:
Getawhisky and Charisma Cat go head-to-head in our feature race from Newbury - live on Sky Sports Racing.

3.00 Newbury - Classy pair Getawhisky and Charisma Cat fancied

The classy duo of Getawhisky and Charisma Cat will be fancied in this Grade 2 BetVictor British EBF "National Hunt" Mares' Novices' Hurdle at Newbury.

The Dan Skelton-trained Getawhisky ran out an impressive winner of a novices' hurdle at Windsor in January before finishing well to claim fourth behind Khrisma at Market Rasen. This step up in distance is sure to suit for a yard still going after every pound to get the trainers' championship over the line.

Charisma Cat and Watamu filled the places that day, but will both have to concede weight to the likely favourite. Charisma Cat has some excellent form including when finishing second to La Conquiere, while Watamu has a similarly progressive profile for Emma Lavelle.

A deep renewal sees lots of alternatives, including the Fergal O'Brien-trained pair Strong Run and Edith Pelham.

A typically competitive renewal of this Goffs Hundred Grand Bumper sees Diamond Street headline a field of 20.

Dan Skelton's gelding was a stylish winner of a Doncaster bumper on debut in January and although the form is untested, he should be in the mix under Harry Skelton.

Lover Desbois, fourth behind Bass Hunter on debut here, improved on that to score at Kempton on his next outing and his trainer Harry Derham explained this has been the plan since.

Keltype made all to win at Chepstow on his second start, while Nicky Henderson's filly Madam Speaker is another to note under Sean Bowen.

A strong Midnite A Next Generation Betting App Handicap is the feature on this eight-race card at Southwell.

Gaeli made a winning debut for Marco Botti and Jack Gilligan when seeing off Fivethousandtoone earlier this month and he must be feared especially if he can step forward despite a 6lbs rise.

Fahrenheit Seven relished the return to the minimum distance when scoring at Newcastle 43 days ago and that form gives him an obvious each-way chance under top pilot Tom Marquand.

Brosay makes appeal on his first start for Charlie Pike and Kylian is another to note on debut for Robert Cowell.

Best of the rest

Returning to handicap company, Storming George will be popular with bettors in the Get Best Odds Guaranteed At BetVictor Novices' Handicap Hurdle at 1.50pm, with the Nicky Henderson-trained Thewoodcorner a likely threat.

Greatwood Gold Cup hero Heltenham looks the one to beat back at Newbury in the 2.25pm BetVictor Home Of The Saturday Superboost Handicap Chase, although Prairie Wolf, Knappers Hill and Twinjets will all fancy their chances in what looks an excellent contest in Berkshire.

At Bangor, Knickerbockerglory and Static are set to lock horns in the feature 3.20pm Virgin Bet Supports Safe Gambling Handicap Hurdle, with Joyeux Machin making an appearance in the Virgin Bet Handicap Chase earlier on the card.

At Newcastle, Rastnet and Alther Walden look the key players in the Midnite: Built For 2026 Not 2006 Handicap before Starglow - who is closely related to Romantic Style - goes to post for the Always Gamble Responsibly At BetMGM Novice Stakes (5.30pm).

Stateside, Fair Grounds plays host to the 10.42pm Grade 2 Louisiana Derby, which features Autobahn and Emerging Market.

Football.title: Im clings on to lead at Valspar Championship as Hovland misses cut
Football.content:
Sungjae Im held onto his lead at the Valspar Championship after rallying on the back nine to edge ahead of David Lipsky.

After missing two straight cuts in his return from a wrist injury, Im shot a two-under 69 to get to nine under par - a stroke ahead of Lipsky in firm and fast conditions on the Copperhead course at Innisbrook.

Im had three bogeys and two birdies in a front-nine 37, then birdied the par-five 11th and par-four 12th. He broke a tie for the lead with a seven-foot birdie putt on the par-three 17th.

The 27-year-old South Korean player has two PGA Tour victories, the 2020 Honda Classic and 2021 Shriners Children's Open.

Lipsky shot 65, playing in the first group of the day off the first tee. Winless on the PGA Tour, he birdied the first two holes and four of the first six, added two more on 10 and 11 and parred the final seven.

Image: Sungjae Im has a one shot lead going into the third round of the Valspar Championship

"It was excellent. I did everything well," Lipsky said. "Missed it in the right spots, holed the putts early on to get some momentum going. That finishing stretch is obviously tough, so able to save a couple pars down on 16 and 18 and really kept the round going."

Chandler Blanchet and Doug Ghim were seven under. Blanchet closed with a bogey for a 66. Ghim shot 67, making an eagle on the par-five 11th, then giving back the strokes with bogeys on 15 and 16.

"Obviously, I wish I would have finished a little bit better," Ghim said. "Two bad holes doesn't really necessarily kind of ruin a good day."

Snedeker had a 72 to drop to five under. The 45-year-old US Presidents Cup captain received a sponsor exemption.

"The good thing is it's playing really tough and tricky this afternoon," Snedeker said. "Try to take a positive from that. Hung in there when things weren't going great."

England's Matt Fitzpatrick, second last week in The Players Championship, was five under with Jordan Smith and Marco Penge.

Brooks Koepka was tied for 10th at four under after a 67 in his return to Innisbrook. He missed a three-foot birdie putt on his final hole.

Defending champion Viktor Hovland missed the cut with rounds of 70 and 75.

Watch round three of The Valspar Championship live on Sky Sports Golf from 11.45am on Saturday. Stream golf contract-free.

Football.title: Devine at the double as Preston beat Stoke
Football.content:
Alfie Devine scored twice as Preston ended a seven-game winless streak with a 3-1 Championship victory over Stoke at Deepdale.

The 21-year-old Tottenham loanee cancelled out a fourth-minute volley from Sorba Thomas with a composed finish in the 15th minute and completed his brace with a delightful dink for Preston's third on the hour mark, just minutes after Milutin Osmajic had headed them in front with his eighth goal of a turbulent season.

Stoke fans were lively from the off - filling three-quarters of the away end - and they were ecstatic when Thomas acrobatically slammed Junior Tchamadeu's cross into the floor and past Daniel Iversen for an early opener.

It was a nightmare start for the out-of-form hosts, however, they bounced back with aplomb 10 minutes later when Stoke over-committed bodies forward.

The visitors left every outfield player in Preston's half as Devine ran onto Andrew Moran's ball from inside his own half before squeezing a shot under the glove of Tommy Simkin to level proceedings.

North End needed that and could have taken the lead in the 23rd minute as Liam Lindsay headed over Devine's corner from an appetising central position.

Visiting supporters were up in arms after 34 minutes as Ben Whiteman clattered Thomas on the halfway line, with Preston looking light in defence, though referee Farai Hallam opted for a yellow card despite calls for a red, given the studs-up nature of the challenge.

Simkin could have done better with the opener and nearly handed Preston a second as he misjudged the flight of Andrew Hughes' cross and the 21-year-old was counting his lucky stars as Osmajic failed to head on target with the goal gaping.

It was one of the best halves of football Preston have had at Deepdale since the turn of the year but Stoke almost equalised early in the second period as Million Manhoef looked set to stab a loose ball past Iversen until Andrija Vukcevic's incredible last-ditch block.

After surviving that scare, Paul Heckingbottom's side soon got a deserved third in the 57th minute with Osmajic making amends for his poor first-half miss by nodding home Lewis Dobbin's pinpoint cross from close-range.

Two quickly became three for the rampant hosts as Osjamic turned provider, slipping Devine in behind a Potters defence that were all at sea and the on-loan Tottenham man delightfully clipped the ball over the advancing Simpkin to bag his eighth goal of the season for the Lilywhites.

Mark Robins' men looked vulnerable to the counter attack throughout and the early second-half substitutes Jesurun Rak-Sakyi, Sam Gallagher and Jun-Ho Bae left them looking even more defensively disjointed, allowing Preston to create that two-goal cushion and see the game out.

The managers

Preston's Paul Heckingbottom:

"Alfie [Devine's] a good player and a good lad. I don't think we've seen the best of him, as good as he's played. There's more to come. He needs pushing but with that as well, you need to understand, he's played a lot of minutes in this league and it's tough.

"You're a young boy, you have peaks and troughs, but where we need to get him is in that consistency of performance because he's got the ability, he's got the brain, and he's got the talent to affect the games on the pitch."

Stoke's Mark Robins:

"It was poor to start with. We just didn't start with the intensity that we needed to play with and played without any quality.

"We've given them chances to get the ball in our box. Not the way we wanted to start the game but they put you under pressure with the way that they play so you have to deal with it.

"We ended up getting our first decent move down the right and Sorba [Thomas] scores. There was an opportunity there to win this game really well tonight and that's the biggest disappointment.

"We've let the supporters down and we know we have."

Football.title: 'Baffling!' Man Utd bemused by penalty calls during draw at Bournemouth
Football.content:
Manchester United boss Michael Carrick said it was "baffling" that Harry Maguire was sent off and gave away a penalty but Bournemouth's Adrien Truffert escaped punishment for an "identical" challenge on Amad Diallo during their 2-2 draw.

Maguire's late foul on Evanilson ultimately proved costly, with Junior Kroupi scoring the resulting spot-kick and United having to hang on with 10 men to take a point from their trip to the south coast.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from Bournemouth’s match against Manchester United in the Premier League

A Bruno Fernandes penalty on the hour mark and James Hill own goal, either side of a fine finish from Ryan Christie, had given United a 2-1 lead before Maguire got his marching orders. But it was an incident just before that Christie goal which left Carrick and United captain Fernandes frustrated.

Amad latched onto a clever pass forward from Fernandes and cut into the box but went to ground when Truffert placed both hands on him. It was similar to Maguire's subsequent foul, which, like Amad, spun Evanilson to the ground.

Monterosa Monterosa , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Monterosa cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Monterosa cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Monterosa cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

"We should have had another penalty," Carrick told Sky Sports. "Obviously, if you get one, you must get the other.

"It's pretty much identical for me, two-hand grab. Either way, [the ref] got one wrong, but to give one and not give the other, I can't get my head around it. I think it's crazy. It's a bit baffling, really.

"Because of that, they score and then it's chaos after that, really. We should have had another penalty and the game would have been totally different."

Fernandes added: "We get a penalty against [us] where more or less it's the same situation as Amad.

"One is rewarded as a penalty, the other one not. I know it's difficult for the referee to give two penalties in the same game for the same team but what I don't understand is why VAR doesn't get involved in that situation.

"Either one is a penalty and the other one too or none of them is a penalty."

However, Sky Sports pundit Jamie Redknapp took a different view.

"It would have been incredibly harsh," he said. "You can see Truffert is being aggressive with him. He does sort of manhandle him a bit, but I just don't think it's enough for it to be a penalty."

Carrick: We are in a good place

Reflecting on the bigger picture, with his team third in the Premier League and well-placed for Champions League qualification, Carrick said: "We're in a good place. We've put ourselves in this position through some good work, good results and good performances.

"We could have had more tonight but it's not the end of the world.

"We've made some improvements. There are things we can improve on, and we can get a lot better.

"I'm happy with the way things have gone, but we're not satisfied. We definitely want more."

Football.title: St Helens cruise to Toulouse win as Bradford beat Huddersfield
Football.content:
St Helens extended their winning streak to four games as a 30-16 victory at Toulouse lift them up to second place in the Betfred Super League.

The Saints owed their win to a dominant first-half performance in which they scored 28 points in the opening 29 minutes.

Agnatius Paasi opened the floodgates after Owen Dagnall's fourth-minute break from a scrum and Jackson Hastings landed the first of five successful kicks.

Saints built on that fast start as Lewis Murphy crossed twice in the space of eight minutes before the midway point of the first period.

Tristan Sailor set up Murphy's second try and the Australian stand-off produced another superb offload to send David Klemmer over.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights of Wakefield Trinity against Leigh Leopards in Super League

Jake Wingfield then set up Deon Cross and Saints were out of sight before the 30-minute mark.

The start of the second half was an arm wrestle, but Toulouse replied through tries from Benjamin Laguerre, Luke Polselli and Tyler Dupree, with Jake Shorrocks landing two goals.

But the boot of Hastings had the final word as he completed a personal 10-point haul.

Nikorima shines on Bulls debut as Giants slayed

Image: Huddersfield remain winless this season

Bottom-placed Huddersfield suffered a fifth consecutive defeat as Bradford stormed to a 26-14 win.

A tight and tryless first half saw the Bulls lead 4-2 as Luke Hooley landed two goals after Olly Russell's successful sixth-minute kick.

The second period was a far more open affair and Giants regained the lead when Connor Wynne knocked on and Tristan Powell was first to the loose ball, with Russell adding the extras.

Bradford took a 16-8 lead as Leon Ruan and Jayden Nikorima powered over in the space of three minutes and Chris Atkin kicked two conversions.

Huddersfield refused to lie down and Jacob Gagai grounded a bouncing ball after the Bulls failed to deal with a high kick, and Russell's conversion reduced the deficit to two points.

But Bradford finished with a flourish as Nikorima went down the short side three minutes from time and fed Hooley, who dived over in the corner.

Bradford sealed the win and moved up to fifth as Nikorima marked his Bulls debut with a second score, with Hooley adding the final gloss.

Football.title: Premier League buildup, United fury, England squad fallout, and more – matchday live
Football.content:
Key events 3h ago Preamble Show key events only Please turn on JavaScript to use this feature

6m ago 06.52 EDT Nottingham Forest and Tottenham both go into their Sunday showdown on the back of European wins. While Spurs’ 3-2 victory over Atletico Madrid wasn’t enough to undo the damage of the 5-2 self-induced tonking in Spain, Forest hit back from their 1-0 first-leg loss at the City Ground to oust Midtjylland on penalties and reach a first European quarter-final for 30 years. Ben Fisher checks in with the Forest camp. Nottingham Forest aim to recreate Midtjylland magic in showdown at Spurs Read more Share

14m ago 06.43 EDT Igor Tudor is a good name, isn’t it. Perhaps the name of a chess player. There’s certainly something 3D chess about his answer here, putting us left-brain dullards to shame as we blithely trot out “six-pointer” for Sunday’s Tottenham v Nottingham Forest clash. Asked if he believed in the concept ahead of the clash between the two relegation-threatened sides, Tudor said: “No. “Because there are a lot of points to play for. Of course, it’s an important game because we are together there, so let’s say not six, but four? “It’s not a final. Maybe not three points, a four-point game but still a lot of points (to go). Especially when we are so close, it’s important, but it will not decide who will (stay up).” Share

22m ago 06.35 EDT Iran: Sardar Azmoun has been omitted from the squad announced for two World Cup warmups after Iranian media reports that the striker had been expelled from the national team for a perceived act of disloyalty to the government. Azmoun, who has scored 57 goals in 91 internationals, was the most notable absentee from a 35-man squad named by Iran head coach, Amir Ghalenoei, on Friday for friendlies against Nigeria and Costa Rica in the Turkish city of Antalya. Sardar Azmoun reportedly left out of Iran squad for disloyalty to government Read more Share

30m ago 06.27 EDT There are three 12.30pm kick-offs in the Championship today. They feature second-placed Middlesbrough and a key showdown between Ipswich (third) and Millwall (fourth). Blackburn v Middlesbrough

Derby v Birmingham

Ipswich v Millwall Leaders Coventry play away at Swansea in a 5.15pm kick-off. Share

51m ago 06.07 EDT More detailed quotes here from a frustrated Manchester United boss Michael Carrick. He was speaking after the 2-2 draw at Bournemouth and wasn’t happy at all with the performance of ref Stuart Atwell. United were given a penalty for Alex Jimenez’s shirt pull on Matheus Cunha but later denied a second spot-kick after Amad Diallo went to ground under a challenge from Adrien Truffert. Bournemouth immediately went up the other end of the pitch and equalised through captain Ryan Christie. Speaking of the incidents involving Cunha and Diallo, Carrick said: “He’s definitely got one of them wrong because he’s given one penalty for us for the same thing that he’s not given one. There’s a two-arm grab. The Matheus one he gives, the second one on Amad he doesn’t, which I think is almost identical, really. “If you have two hands on someone in the box and they go over and they’re in control of the ball, for me it’s two penalties. It will be interesting to see which one they acknowledge is wrong: the one we got or the one we didn’t get. “It’s a huge moment. They don’t give it, they go down the other end and score and then it becomes all of a sudden, ‘oh, it needs to be a bigger penalty to overturn’, just because they scored, when actually it’s a penalty and it should be a penalty if you’ve already given one. “It’s baffling really to make sense of that. And because they score, the game flips a little bit and changes. We defended with the 10 men after all that very well. But the penalty one is just astonishing, I have to say. One of them must be wrong.” Share

1h ago 05.58 EDT Paul Robinson. The former Leeds and England goalkeeper? The fella off Neighbours, who went to No.16 in the UK Singles Chart with “Don’t It Make You Feel Good”? Nope, we’re talking the man who controversially replaced Alan Shearer in the Newcastle line-up. Ahead of the Tyne-Wear derby with Sunderland, Harry De Cosemo catches up with the former Magpies striker. ‘Newcastle fans booed me. Sunderland fans booed me’: the striker who replaced Shearer Read more Share

1h ago 05.49 EDT Expecting big things from your fantasy teams this weekend? I’m kicking myself for not captaining Bruno Fernandes, who banked 13pts (26 if he was your skipper) at Bournemouth after a goal, an assist and the maximum 3 Bonus points. I’ve given the armband to Cole Palmer in the belief that Chelsea score bundles on the road and he might have one of his crazy weeks and bang in a hat-trick. Everton aren’t that great at home, are they, and Palmer (16% owned) is a bit of a differential. Share

1h ago 05.41 EDT Carabao Cup final: Of course, the Premier League’s top two – Arsenal and Manchester City – don’t have a top-flight game this weekend as they’re battling it out for the first silverware of the season on Sunday. The Wembley showdown kicks of at 4.30pm GMT. The bookies make Arsenal favourites. Let’s get in the mood with a quiz. I managed a reasonable 10 out of 15 after, admittedly, fluking a couple. The big League Cup final quiz Read more Share

2h ago 05.22 EDT An email. And not a very happy one. Dave Estherby (who may have opted to ignore the right side of his bed when getting up this morning), says: “Any lawyers out there that can see if I have a case against Sky Sports for relieving me of a month’s subscription charge and only providing two weeks of (PL) football? Not the first time it’s happened, I fail to see how England’s already ambitious World Cup campaign in going to benefit from friendlies against (with respect) Uruguay and Japan - especially if anyone gets injured. With two F1 races now cancelled and RoryMac likely crocked for the Masters it’s gonna be a pretty boring sporting April now. (Before anyone starts I’m aware there is WSL and EFL on and that the Middle East situation isn’t funny; just allow me my rant plz, thanks).” Oof. I’ll just clear up Rory’s back. He played all four rounds of last week’s Players Championship and said later: “Had that little back issue at Bay Hill, which threw a spanner in the works, but I’ve recovered from that pretty quickly. I’m really pleased with how my body responded to that little setback.”



But, getting this back to football, Dave does highlight a point about the strange hole in the Premier League programme. Manchester United played last night: the 20th of March. Their next Premier League game is home to Leeds on the 13th of April. Share Updated at 05.22 EDT

2h ago 05.14 EDT Liverpool will have to cope without Mo Salah and Alisson for the trip to Brighton. Both have muscle injuries. But here’s some much better Liverpool fitness news: Alexander Isak is on his way back. The £125m British record signing will be available – in some capacity – for Liverpool’s Champions League quarter-final against Paris Saint-Germain, says boss Arne Slot. Arne Slot confirms Isak will be fit for Liverpool’s trip to PSG next month Read more Share

Follow the Women’s Asian Cup final between Japan and Australia in Sydney here. It kicks off in five minutes and, as Jonathan says: “It is the most significant fixture involving an Australian national team since Ange Postecoglou led the Socceroos to continental glory in 2015. The hosts will have the majority of a 60,000+ crowd behind them but they will enter the decider as underdogs. Japan have reached at least the semi-finals of every Asian Cup since 1986, winning two out of the past three titles.” Japan v Australia: Women’s Asian Cup 2026 final - live Read more Share

Leipzig were 4-0 up at half-time in a match which saw Brajan Gruda and Christoph Baumgartner both score twice.
